decisions.relationship_evidence: Sequencing and phylogenetic analysis identified multiple XBB.1.16 lineages and independent introductions into Bangkok.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 genomic-epidemiology application in Bangkok.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper presents a global Nextstrain build for analyzing West Nile virus emergence, spread, and lineage dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides a publicly available pathogen-genomic analysis build.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome comparisons were used to link human M. bovis cases with deer and cattle isolates and assess transmission.
Phylogenetic and phylodynamic analyses reconstructed viral introductions, southward spread, and cross-species transmission among wild birds.
Genomic and epidemiologic data, phylogenies, and discrete-trait analysis were jointly used to infer introductions and transmission within long-term-care facilities.
Sequence-based subtyping supported molecular tracing and assessment of relationships among late BVDV outbreaks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete sequence-based outbreak-tracing application in cattle.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Genomic sequencing, phylogenetics, and a structured-coalescent model reconstructed AMR E. coli transmission between humans and canines.
Whole-genome sequencing and phylodynamic analysis inferred the origin, introductions, and community spread of monkeypox virus in Paraguay.
Sequencing and phylogenetic analysis assessed introductions and ongoing intercity transmission of Candida auris in Colombia.
Phylodynamic analysis integrated bird-movement and viral-genome data to reconstruct global avian-influenza lineage dispersal.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of pathogen dispersal.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Population genomics and phylogenetic analysis characterized circulation and inferred long-term horizontal and vertical transmission of resistant E. coli mechanisms on farms.
Genomic analysis identified the introduction and subsequent geographic clustering of an E. coli ST131 sublineage in Wales.
Whole-genome data were combined with household epidemiology to distinguish index-case transmission from outside introductions.
A mosquito-derived DENV-2 genome was compared phylogenetically with other genomes to assess relatedness and possible introduction into Ecuador.
Whole-genome clusters and detainee location histories were combined to identify probable SARS-CoV-2 transmission within a jail.
Whole-genome, phylogenetic, phylodynamic, and spatial analyses identified recent drug-resistant tuberculosis transmission clusters.
Bayesian phylogenetic and population-dynamic analyses were applied to available Nipah virus genomes and partial sequences.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete viral phylogenetic and phylodynamic application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome and pan-genomic analyses characterized phylogenetic relationships and dissemination of invasive MRSA clones in Paraguay.
Phylogenetic, coalescent, and transmission-network analyses reconstructed the origin, dissemination, and risk-group migration of HIV-1 CRF65_cpx.
Whole-genome phylogenetics linked human and cat isolates and inferred independent emergence followed by animal and human transmission.
Bayesian phylodynamic analysis was used to infer wild-bird transmission and long-term maintenance of H5 avian influenza viruses.
The study directly compared cholera transmission estimates from genomic phylodynamic models with established case-count models.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmark of genomic phylodynamic inference against epidemiological modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review explains WGS advantages over traditional S. suis typing but does not introduce, apply, critique, diagnose, correct, or implement the exact concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological overview of WGS-based epidemiological characterization.

decisions.relationship_evidence: WGS and phylogenomic comparison were used to characterize changing gonococcal lineages and antimicrobial resistance in Brazil.
Whole-genome sequencing and phylogenetic analysis compared clinical and chicken-origin Salmonella populations and resistance-gene dissemination.
Conventional and genomic epidemiology were used to establish a single outbreak origin and identify transmission vectors between prison modules.
Whole-genome phylogenetic analyses were used to identify cholera transmission patterns and co-circulating lineages across eastern and southern Africa.
Nationwide WGS and comparative genomics characterized related carbapenem-resistant E. coli reservoirs in healthy and hospitalized populations.
The prospective study explicitly evaluated how routine genomic epidemiology changed hospital infection-control investigations and decisions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of genomic epidemiology's operational impact.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Global WGS, MLST, and core-genome MLST characterized the diversity and geographic clustering of Salmonella Orion.
Bayesian phylogenetics identified HIV transmission clusters and estimated cluster age and reproductive numbers across the US–Mexico border.
Whole-genome phylogenetic and phylodynamic analyses inferred host transitions and livestock-to-wildlife spillovers of S. aureus.
Genome analysis and a global database comparison characterized dissemination patterns of blaNDM-carrying Klebsiella variicola.
Whole-genome SNP analysis identified putative community and household transmission clusters of S. pyogenes.
Longitudinal whole-genome sequencing and phylogenetic reconstruction characterized intra-host evolution and possible inter-host spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 evolutionary and transmission application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome and spatiotemporal analyses reconstructed chikungunya lineage introductions and dissemination across Argentina.
Extensive genomic sequencing analyzed CVA10 lineage evolution, recombination, and changing epidemiological dominance.
Whole-genome analysis tested for spatiotemporal clustering of non-faecium, non-faecalis enterococci and found no single strain.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic-epidemiology application retaining a null transmission result.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The chapter summarizes established genomic-epidemiology procedures but does not itself introduce, apply, diagnose, correct, critique, or implement the exact concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methods guidance covering outbreak detection and epidemiological characterization.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Short- and long-read genomic analyses distinguished plasmid-mediated horizontal transmission from clonal transmission of carbapenemase genes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete plasmid and bacterial transmission application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Phylogenetic genomic analysis estimated prevalence and transmission pathways of fish-pathogenic M. pseudoshottsii.
The WHO Tricycle protocol, isolate sequencing, and SNP comparisons were applied to assess intersector circulation of ESBL E. coli and plasmids.
Phylogenomic analysis characterized the population structure of a large Blastomyces outbreak.
New mpox genomes and Bayesian phylogenetics reconstructed the epidemic's emergence time, geographic origin, and dissemination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete mpox emergence and transmission reconstruction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome and phylogenetic analyses identified SARS-CoV-2 importation and subsequent local transmission in Tamil Nadu.
The study compared genome-fragment lengths for typing accuracy, phylogenetic concordance, and phylodynamic population inference.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation of sequence lengths used in phylodynamic analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Maximum-likelihood and BEAST phylodynamic trees were used to characterize rhinovirus evolution and cocirculating clades.
Whole-genome clustering and epidemiologic investigation assessed suspected healthcare-associated M. abscessus transmission and found it rare.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete investigation preserving a largely null transmission result.
named_source_mentions: Healthcare-Associated Links in Transmission of NTM (HALT NTM) study

decisions.relationship_evidence: The scoping review systematically evaluated the data, functionality, visualization, and computational architecture of six genomic-epidemiology dashboards.
Maximum-likelihood and Bayesian phylogenetics inferred Rift Valley fever virus lineage ancestry and regional spread.
Time-scaled phylogeny and population-dynamic analyses reconstructed CV-A6 ancestry, evolution, and covert circulation.
Whole-genome bioinformatics and phylogenomics characterized A. baumannii lineages, resistance, and transmission-relevant genomic patterns.
Microbial genomics and phylogenomics showed that two independently introduced V. cholerae strains caused the Lebanese outbreak.
Molecular networks, time-resolved phylogenies, and Bayesian inference identified HIV transmission clusters, hubs, and dispersal patterns.
Longitudinal patient and wastewater sequencing with recombination-adjusted phylogenies characterized hospital transmission reservoirs and dynamics.
Whole-genome phylogenetic comparison investigated the origin and relatedness of human mecC-carrying S. aureus isolates.
Whole-genome phylogenetics linked poliovirus detections, reconstructed three lineages, and connected geographically separated outbreak sites.
Genome sequencing and phylogenetic analysis identified nosocomial, community, pig, and interspecies clonal transmission.
Parasite genomic relatedness integrated with survey data identified spatially and temporally focal malaria transmission clusters.
Phylogenomic and Bayesian population analyses characterized geographic clustering and population dynamics of mpox lineage B.1.
Population genomics integrated with demographic and travel data characterized contrasting spatial transmission and dispersal patterns of two malaria species.
Genomic analyses inferred Candida auris introductions and interhospital and patient-to-patient transmission in the UK.
Whole-genome and phylogenetic analyses characterized extrapulmonary NTM species, sequence types, resistance, and genomic relationships.
A retrospective analysis of 2,274 genomes used genomic-epidemiology approaches to characterize SARS-CoV-2 lineages and evolutionary trends in Morocco.
named_source_mentions: STROBE (Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology) guidelines

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrated epidemiological and phylodynamic analyses reconstructed bidirectional international transmission and variant spread in Italy.
Whole-genome phylogenetic analysis assessed relationships and transmission-relevant resistance patterns among non-typhoidal Salmonella isolates.
Genomic analysis reconstructed sources of SARS-CoV-2 importation into Haiti and exportation to other regions.
The paper provides updated curated genomic databases, population-structure visualization, a graphical interface, and an API in EnteroBase.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software-platform implementation supporting bacterial genomic epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Viral sequencing and phylogenetic analysis identified clustered and multiple enterovirus lineages in homeless shelters.
Whole-genome surveillance characterized RSV genomic landscapes and identified clusters of identical genomes in Minnesota.
A simulation study quantitatively compared structured-coalescent and multitype birth-death estimates of pathogen migration rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance benchmark of two structured phylodynamic methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing characterized the distribution of A. baumannii sequence types and resistance genes across Vietnamese hospitals.
Whole-genome analysis characterized the distribution and plasmid-mediated transmission of tigecycline-resistance genes across China.
Time-resolved HIV phylogenies and coalescent patterns were used to identify subepidemics with differing transmission trajectories.
Whole genomes and donor-recipient pairs reconstructed a single-source SARS-CoV-2 outbreak and quantified its transmission bottleneck.
Bayesian phylogenetic, molecular-dating, and population-dynamic analyses reconstructed Omicron introductions and expansion in Pakistan.
Whole-genome and epidemiological data distinguished household transmissions from outside SARS-CoV-2 introductions.
The paper develops laboratory-capacity guidance for genomic surveillance, but does not explicitly develop or apply genomic transmission reconstruction itself.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The framework concerns surveillance capacity rather than the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Multicountry genomic surveillance identified multidrug-resistant high-risk E. coli and K. pneumoniae clones and their resistance diversity.
Whole genomes, patient-transfer records, and temporal analysis reconstructed reimportation and person-to-person Candida parapsilosis transmission.
The study used genomic epidemiology to characterize Salmonella Panama sublineages and infer frequent travel-associated importation into Australia.
Phylogenetic and Bayesian phylodynamic analyses estimated RSV clustering, ancestry, diversity, and population history in Pakistan.
The review directly addresses data-quality, sequencing, interpretive, and expertise limitations affecting genomic surveillance and phylogenetic interpretation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological perspective with limitations and recommendations for phylodynamic analytics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome analysis identified major fluoroquinolone-resistant Shigella clusters associated with transmission among men who have sex with men.
Whole-genome analysis characterized national and international hypervirulent Group A Streptococcus lineages and cross-border transmission.
Whole-genome analysis characterized circulating Campylobacter sequence types, diversity, virulence, and resistance in West Bengal.
The study explicitly developed a pangenome graph-based workflow for characterizing and clustering blaGES-5-associated integrons.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development demonstrated in a global genomic-epidemiology case study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Global phylogenetic and Bayesian analyses reconstructed the emergence and evolution of P. aeruginosa ST111 clades.
Genomic and phylogenetic analyses identified SARS-CoV-2 recombinant transmission clusters in Botswana.
Whole-genome and Bayesian analyses identified two independently emerging C. difficile ST81 sublineages across Chinese hospitals.
Outbreak sequencing distinguished the Peruvian Oropouche lineage from Brazil and linked it to strains from Colombia and Ecuador.
Whole-genome and evolutionary analyses characterized changing pertussis genotypes under vaccine selection across periods and regions.
Routine sequencing, phylogenetic trees, and phylodynamic analysis characterized SARS-CoV-2 waves and cross-border importation and exportation in Guinea.
Phylogenetic and phylodynamic analyses reconstructed Omicron importations, exportations, and distinct transmission pathways in Florida.
Metagenomic recovery and Bayesian phylogenetics inferred a South American introduction followed by local dengue transmission in Lombardy.
Whole-genome and core-SNP comparisons assessed whether ESBL-producing E. coli strains were shared between humans and a local river.
WGS and Bayesian stochastic models reconstructed transmission networks, seven introductions, and secondary infection chains in a hospital unit.
Phylodynamic analysis of genomic data inferred repeated human-to-deer spillovers and probable onward transmission in deer.
Population genomics reconstructed phylogenetic relationships, spatial-genetic correlations, ancestry, and introduction of E. amylovora into Korea.
A timed phylogeny and structured-coalescent model quantified persistent cattle-to-human E. coli O157:H7 lineage transmission.
